text: The video shows a stricture in the sigmoid colon. The surgeon initially attempts local shaving of the lesion but finds it too deep, leading to the decision to perform a bowel resection of the sigmoid colon.

The surgeon performs an end-to-end anastomosis of the sigmoid colon using a stapler. The stapler is inserted through the rectum, approximating the two bowel segments, and a staple line is fired circumferentially to create a seal. Fluorescence imaging is used to confirm good vascularization of the re-approximated segments.
The surgeon addresses endometriosis on the pelvic sidewall, carefully excising affected tissue to alleviate the patient's symptoms and improve fertility prospects.

The surgeon removes visible endometriosis from the underside of the left ovary and the right ovary, which is adhered to the pelvic sidewall. Fluorescence imaging is used to identify the ureter, aiding in the precision of the procedure.

The surgeon highlights the ureter with green fluorescence after flushing it with icy green dye via cystoscopy and catheterization. On the underside of the ovary, a small endometrioma is identified and excised. The excised sample is placed in a bag and extracted through a small incision.
